revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1738774]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |TITLE = Kenmet |NAME = V31:N35-X1 G14-N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME2 = V31:N35-T34-G17-N14-X1:H8-C10
Kenmet
''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ}} of the [[:en:Celestial sphere|Celestial Sphere]], first in the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|of the Middle Kingdom]] as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]. The association frequently made in the [[:en:Secondary source|Secondary Literature]] between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on [[:en:Engraved gem|Gemmae]] can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ}} (''Ba''), of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian mythology)]], and of [[:en:Isis|Isis]]. During the [[:en:Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|Third Intermediate Period]], Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as [[:en:Re-Hor-achti|Ra-Horakhty]], {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Khepri|Chepri]], [[:en:Atum|Atum]], and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Kenmet (Egyptian mythology)]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Kenmet (Egyptian mythology)]] [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Kenmet (Egyptian mythology)]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Kenmet (Egyptian mythology)]] [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1738774]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |TITLE = Kenmet |NAME = V31:N35-X1 G14-N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME2 = V31:N35-T34-G17-N14-X1:H8-C10
Kenmet
''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the [[:en:Celestial sphere|Celestial Sphere]], first in the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|of the Middle Kingdom]] as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]. The association frequently made in the [[:en:Secondary source|Secondary Literature]] between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on [[:en:Engraved gem|Gemmae]] can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian mythology)]], and of [[:en:Isis|Isis]]. During the [[:en:Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|Third Intermediate Period]], Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as [[:en:Re-Hor-achti|Ra-Horakhty]], {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Khepri|Chepri]], [[:en:Atum|Atum]], and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Kenmet (Egyptian mythology)]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Kenmet (Egyptian mythology)]] [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}}

In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1738774]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |TITLE = Kenmet |NAME = V31:N35-X1 G14-N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME2 = V31:N35-T34-G17-N14-X1:H8-C10
Kenmet
''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the [[:en:Celestial sphere|Celestial Sphere]], first in the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|of the Middle Kingdom]] as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]. The association frequently made in the [[:en:Secondary source|Secondary Literature]] between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on [[:en:Engraved gem|Gemmae]] can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian mythology)]], and of [[:en:Isis|Isis]]. During the [[:en:Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|Third Intermediate Period]], Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as [[:en:Re-Hor-achti|Ra-Horakhty]], {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Khepri|Chepri]], [[:en:Atum|Atum]], and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}}

Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1738774]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |TITLE = Kenmet |NAME = V31:N35-X1 G14-N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME2 = V31:N35-T34-G17-N14-X1:H8-C10
Kenmet
''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the [[:en:Celestial sphere|Celestial Sphere]], first in the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|of the Middle Kingdom]] as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]. The association frequently made in the [[:en:Secondary source|Secondary Literature]] between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on [[:en:Engraved gem|Gemmae]] can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian mythology)]], and of [[:en:Isis|Isis]]. During the [[:en:Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|Third Intermediate Period]], Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as [[:en:Re-Hor-achti|Ra-Horakhty]], {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Khepri|Chepri]], [[:en:Atum|Atum]], and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}}

In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}}

revision.text:
[[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1738774]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |TITLE = Kenmet |NAME = V31:N35-X1 G14-N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME2 = V31:N35-T34-G17-N14-X1:H8-C10
Kenmet
''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the [[:en:Celestial sphere|Celestial Sphere]], first in the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|of the Middle Kingdom]] as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]. The association frequently made in the [[:en:Secondary source|Secondary Literature]] between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on [[:en:Engraved gem|Gemmae]] can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian mythology)]], and of [[:en:Isis|Isis]]. During the [[:en:Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|Third Intermediate Period]], Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as [[:en:Re-Hor-achti|Ra-Horakhty]], {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Khepri|Chepri]], [[:en:Atum|Atum]], and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1738774]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |TITLE = Kenmet |NAME = V31:N35-X1 G14-N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME2 = V31:N35-T34-G17-N14-X1:H8-C10
Kenmet
''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the [[:en:Celestial sphere|Celestial Sphere]], first in the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|of the Middle Kingdom]] as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]. The association frequently made in the [[:en:Secondary source|Secondary Literature]] between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on [[:en:Engraved gem|Gemmae]] can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian mythology)]], and of [[:en:Isis|Isis]]. During the [[:en:Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|Third Intermediate Period]], Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as [[:en:Re-Hor-achti|Ra-Horakhty]], {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Khepri|Chepri]], [[:en:Atum|Atum]], and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1738774]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |TITLE = Kenmet |NAME = V31:N35-X1 G14-N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME2 = V31:N35-T34-G17-N14-X1:H8-C10
Kenmet
''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the [[:en:Celestial sphere|Celestial Sphere]], first in the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|of the Middle Kingdom]] as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]. The association frequently made in the [[:en:Secondary source|Secondary Literature]] between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on [[:en:Engraved gem|Gemmae]] can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian mythology)]], and of [[:en:Isis|Isis]]. During the [[:en:Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|Third Intermediate Period]], Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as [[:en:Re-Hor-achti|Ra-Horakhty]], {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Khepri|Chepri]], [[:en:Atum|Atum]], and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1738774]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |TITLE = Kenmet |NAME = V31:N35-X1 G14-N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME2 = V31:N35-T34-G17-N14-X1:H8-C10
Kenmet
''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1738774]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |TITLE = Kenmet |NAME = V31:N35-X1 G14-N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME2 = V31:N35-T34-G17-N14-X1:H8-C10
Kenmet
''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the [[:en:Celestial sphere|Celestial Sphere]], first in the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|of the Middle Kingdom]] as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]. The association frequently made in the [[:en:Secondary source|Secondary Literature]] between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on [[:en:Engraved gem|Gemmae]] can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian mythology)]], and of [[:en:Isis|Isis]]. During the [[:en:Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|Third Intermediate Period]], Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as [[:en:Re-Hor-achti|Ra-Horakhty]], {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Khepri|Chepri]], [[:en:Atum|Atum]], and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]]ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the [[:en:Celestial sphere|Celestial Sphere]], first in the [[:en:Coffin Texts|Coffin Texts]] of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|of the Middle Kingdom]] as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]. The association frequently made in the [[:en:Secondary source|Secondary Literature]] between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on [[:en:Engraved gem|Gemmae]] can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian mythology)]], and of [[:en:Isis|Isis]]. During the [[:en:Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|Third Intermediate Period]], Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as [[:en:Re-Hor-achti|Ra-Horakhty]], {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Khepri|Chepri]], [[:en:Atum|Atum]], and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
