The association frequently made in the [[:en:Secondary source|Secondary Literature]] between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity [[:en:Khnum|Chnum]] is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on [[:en:Engraved gem|Gemmae]] can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of [[:en:Ra|Re (Egyptian mythology)]], and of [[:en:Isis|Isis]]. During the [[:en:Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|Third Intermediate Period]], Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as [[:en:Re-Hor-achti|Ra-Horakhty]], {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Khepri|Chepri]], [[:en:Atum|Atum]], and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] as the bearer of the [[:en:Eye of Ra|Eye of Re]]. === Mythological Aspects === In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of [[:en:Horus|Horus]]''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on [[:en:Season of the Inundation|15. Achet IV]] to bring "the time of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]" ([[:en:Season|Season]] [[:en:October|October]]). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of [[:en:Letopolis|Letopolis]] (''Hem'', northwest of [[:en:Memphis, Egypt|Memphis (Egypt)]]), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the [[:en:Sacrifice|Sacrificial Cult]]. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine [[:en:Star|Star]]s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]] et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * [[:en:Joachim Friedrich Quack|Joachim Friedrich Quack]], [[:en:Kim Ryholt|Kim Ryholt]]: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Kenmet
''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}}ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the {{ill|Celestial sphere|en|Celestial sphere|WD=Q12134|lt=Celestial Sphere}}, first in the {{ill|Coffin Texts|en|Coffin Texts|WD=Q532105|lt=Coffin Texts}} of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=of the Middle Kingdom}} as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut}} as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|WD=Q1519552|lt=Flooding of the Nile}}. The association frequently made in the {{ill|Secondary source|en|Secondary source|WD=Q905511|lt=Secondary Literature}} between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity {{ill|Khnum|en|Khnum|WD=Q183097|lt=Chnum}} is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on {{ill|Engraved gem|en|Engraved gem|WD=Q1501187|lt=Gemmae}} can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re (Egyptian mythology)}}, and of {{ill|Isis|en|Isis|WD=Q79876|lt=Isis}}. During the {{ill|Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|en|Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|WD=Q212728|lt=Third Intermediate Period}}, Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as {{ill|Re-Hor-achti|en|Re-Hor-achti|WD=Q1418931|lt=Ra-Horakhty}}, {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Khepri|en|Khepri|WD=Q215940|lt=Chepri}}, {{ill|Atum|en|Atum|WD=Q170569|lt=Atum}}, and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|WD=Q930167|lt=Sopdet}} as the bearer of the {{ill|Eye of Ra|en|Eye of Ra|WD=Q760218|lt=Eye of Re}}. === Mythological Aspects === In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}}, Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}}''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=15. Achet IV}} to bring "the time of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris}}" ({{ill|Season|en|Season|WD=Q24384|lt=Season}} {{ill|October|en|October|WD=Q124|lt=October}}). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of {{ill|Letopolis|en|Letopolis|WD=Q1013648|lt=Letopolis}} (''Hem'', northwest of {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis (Egypt)}}), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the {{ill|Sacrifice|en|Sacrifice|WD=Q179723|lt=Sacrificial Cult}}. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star}}s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}} et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * {{ill|Joachim Friedrich Quack|en|Joachim Friedrich Quack|WD=Q1396207|lt=Joachim Friedrich Quack}}, {{ill|Kim Ryholt|en|Kim Ryholt|WD=Q443108|lt=Kim Ryholt}}: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}}ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the {{ill|Celestial sphere|en|Celestial sphere|WD=Q12134|lt=Celestial Sphere}}, first in the {{ill|Coffin Texts|en|Coffin Texts|WD=Q532105|lt=Coffin Texts}} of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=of the Middle Kingdom}} as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut}} as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|WD=Q1519552|lt=Flooding of the Nile}}. The association frequently made in the {{ill|Secondary source|en|Secondary source|WD=Q905511|lt=Secondary Literature}} between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity {{ill|Khnum|en|Khnum|WD=Q183097|lt=Chnum}} is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on {{ill|Engraved gem|en|Engraved gem|WD=Q1501187|lt=Gemmae}} can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re (Egyptian mythology)}}, and of {{ill|Isis|en|Isis|WD=Q79876|lt=Isis}}. During the {{ill|Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|en|Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|WD=Q212728|lt=Third Intermediate Period}}, Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as {{ill|Re-Hor-achti|en|Re-Hor-achti|WD=Q1418931|lt=Ra-Horakhty}}, {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Khepri|en|Khepri|WD=Q215940|lt=Chepri}}, {{ill|Atum|en|Atum|WD=Q170569|lt=Atum}}, and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|WD=Q930167|lt=Sopdet}} as the bearer of the {{ill|Eye of Ra|en|Eye of Ra|WD=Q760218|lt=Eye of Re}}. === Mythological Aspects === In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}}, Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}}''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=15. Achet IV}} to bring "the time of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris}}" ({{ill|Season|en|Season|WD=Q24384|lt=Season}} {{ill|October|en|October|WD=Q124|lt=October}}). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of {{ill|Letopolis|en|Letopolis|WD=Q1013648|lt=Letopolis}} (''Hem'', northwest of {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis (Egypt)}}), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the {{ill|Sacrifice|en|Sacrifice|WD=Q179723|lt=Sacrificial Cult}}. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star}}s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}} et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so‑called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * {{ill|Joachim Friedrich Quack|en|Joachim Friedrich Quack|WD=Q1396207|lt=Joachim Friedrich Quack}}, {{ill|Kim Ryholt|en|Kim Ryholt|WD=Q443108|lt=Kim Ryholt}}: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}}ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the {{ill|Celestial sphere|en|Celestial sphere|WD=Q12134|lt=Celestial Sphere}}, first in the {{ill|Coffin Texts|en|Coffin Texts|WD=Q532105|lt=Coffin Texts}} of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=of the Middle Kingdom}} as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut}} as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|WD=Q1519552|lt=Flooding of the Nile}}. The association frequently made in the {{ill|Secondary source|en|Secondary source|WD=Q905511|lt=Secondary Literature}} between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity {{ill|Khnum|en|Khnum|WD=Q183097|lt=Chnum}} is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on {{ill|Engraved gem|en|Engraved gem|WD=Q1501187|lt=Gemmae}} can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re (Egyptian mythology)}}, and of {{ill|Isis|en|Isis|WD=Q79876|lt=Isis}}. During the {{ill|Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|en|Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|WD=Q212728|lt=Third Intermediate Period}}, Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as {{ill|Re-Hor-achti|en|Re-Hor-achti|WD=Q1418931|lt=Ra-Horakhty}}, {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Khepri|en|Khepri|WD=Q215940|lt=Chepri}}, {{ill|Atum|en|Atum|WD=Q170569|lt=Atum}}, and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|WD=Q930167|lt=Sopdet}} as the bearer of the {{ill|Eye of Ra|en|Eye of Ra|WD=Q760218|lt=Eye of Re}}. === Mythological Aspects === In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}}, Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}}''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=15. Achet IV}} to bring "the time of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris}}" ({{ill|Season|en|Season|WD=Q24384|lt=Season}} {{ill|October|en|October|WD=Q124|lt=October}}). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of {{ill|Letopolis|en|Letopolis|WD=Q1013648|lt=Letopolis}} (''Hem'', northwest of {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis (Egypt)}}), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the {{ill|Sacrifice|en|Sacrifice|WD=Q179723|lt=Sacrificial Cult}}. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star}}s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}} et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * {{ill|Joachim Friedrich Quack|en|Joachim Friedrich Quack|WD=Q1396207|lt=Joachim Friedrich Quack}}, {{ill|Kim Ryholt|en|Kim Ryholt|WD=Q443108|lt=Kim Ryholt}}: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * {{ill|Joachim Friedrich Quack|en|Joachim Friedrich Quack|WD=Q1396207|lt=Joachim Friedrich Quack|1=Joachim Friedrich Quack}}, {{ill|Kim Ryholt|en|Kim Ryholt|WD=Q443108|lt=Kim Ryholt|1=Kim Ryholt}}: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

[[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] {{Infobox deity |TITLE = Kenmet |NAME = V31:N35-X1 G14-N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} |NAME2 = V31:N35-T34-G17-N14-X1:H8-C10
Kenmet
''Knmt''
''[Translation not possible]'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|de|Gr.-röm. Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kenmet''' (also '''Kenmut, Chnoubis, Chnoumis, Chnouphis, Knuphis''') is attested as an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}}ian {{ill|Serpent Deity|de|Schlangengottheit|ja|蛇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Serpent Deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヘビ|1=Snake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2102}} of the {{ill|Celestial sphere|en|Celestial sphere|WD=Q12134|lt=Celestial Sphere|1=Celestial sphere}}, first in the {{ill|Coffin Texts|en|Coffin Texts|WD=Q532105|lt=Coffin Texts|1=Coffin Texts}} of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=of the Middle Kingdom|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} as well as in the associated {{ill|Diagonal Star Clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|ja|対角星時計|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal Star Clock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the same time, Kenmet appears in the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut|1=Book of Nut}} as the eponymous {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the role of the constellation responsible for the {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|WD=Q1519552|lt=Flooding of the Nile|1=Flooding of the Nile}}. The association frequently made in the {{ill|Secondary source|en|Secondary source|WD=Q905511|lt=Secondary Literature|1=Secondary source}} between the names ''Chnoubis, Chnoumis'' (and similar) and the deity {{ill|Khnum|en|Khnum|WD=Q183097|lt=Chnum|1=Khnum}} is untenable, since, for example, the so‐called ''Chnouphis sign'' found on {{ill|Engraved gem|en|Engraved gem|WD=Q1501187|lt=Gemmae|1=Engraved gem}} can be attributed to {{ill|Decan Kenmet|de|Kenmet (Dekan)|ja|ケンメト（デカン）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan Kenmet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Der Himmel über Esna''. p. 59. == Background == === Representations and Functions === [[File:Ägyptischer Maler um 1360 v. Chr. 001.jpg|thumb|Depiction of Isis in the New Kingdom]] In the Middle Kingdom, Kenmet is iconographically represented both as an erect snake, crossed by three smaller snakes, and as a snake with a falcon or human head. At the same time, Kenmet embodies the attributes of a {{ill|Ram|de|Widders|ja|牡羊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ram|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒツジ|1=Sheep|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7368}} (''Ba''), of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|WD=Q1252904|lt=Re (Egyptian mythology)|1=Ra}}, and of {{ill|Isis|en|Isis|WD=Q79876|lt=Isis|1=Isis}}. During the {{ill|Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|en|Third Intermediate Period of Egypt|WD=Q212728|lt=Third Intermediate Period|1=Third Intermediate Period of Egypt}}, Kenmet venerates the activities of {{ill|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|de|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|ja|アメン・レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with his manifestation as {{ill|Re-Hor-achti|en|Re-Hor-achti|WD=Q1418931|lt=Ra-Horakhty|1=Ra-Horakhty}}, {{ill|Horakhty|de|Harachte|ja|ハラハテ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horakhty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Khepri|en|Khepri|WD=Q215940|lt=Chepri|1=Khepri}}, {{ill|Atum|en|Atum|WD=Q170569|lt=Atum|1=Atum}}, and {{ill|Harmachis|de|Harmachis|ja|ハルマキス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harmachis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kenmet's function as Isis is equated with the character traits of {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|WD=Q930167|lt=Sopdet|1=Sopdet}} as the bearer of the {{ill|Eye of Ra|en|Eye of Ra|WD=Q760218|lt=Eye of Re|1=Eye of Ra}}. === Mythological Aspects === In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}, Kenmet, together with the two celestial turtles, represents the ''Life of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}''. Kenmet is the deity who departs on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=15. Achet IV|1=Season of the Inundation}} to bring "the time of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}" ({{ill|Season|en|Season|WD=Q24384|lt=Season|1=Season}} {{ill|October|en|October|WD=Q124|lt=October|1=October}}). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of {{ill|Letopolis|en|Letopolis|WD=Q1013648|lt=Letopolis|1=Letopolis}} (''Hem'', northwest of {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis (Egypt)|1=Memphis, Egypt}}), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the {{ill|Sacrifice|en|Sacrifice|WD=Q179723|lt=Sacrificial Cult|1=Sacrifice}}. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}}s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}} et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * {{ill|Joachim Friedrich Quack|en|Joachim Friedrich Quack|WD=Q1396207|lt=Joachim Friedrich Quack|1=Joachim Friedrich Quack}}, {{ill|Kim Ryholt|en|Kim Ryholt|WD=Q443108|lt=Kim Ryholt|1=Kim Ryholt}}: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''. Museum Tusculanum Press, Copenhagen 2006, ISBN 87-635-0405-7. == References == {{translated page|de|Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)|version=186404581|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Kenmet (ägyptische Mythologie)]] [[ja:ケンメト]] [[de:Kenmet]] {{wikidata link|Q1738774}} [[Category:Egyptian gods]] [[Category:Snake deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Kenmet is the deity who departs on {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|qid=Q770568|lt=15. Achet IV|1=Season of the Inundation}} to bring "the time of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}" ({{ill|Season|en|Season|qid=Q24384|lt=Season|1=Season}} {{ill|October|en|October|qid=Q124|lt=October|1=October}}). In the Greco-Roman period, Kenmet, as the Horus of {{ill|Letopolis|en|Letopolis|qid=Q1013648|lt=Letopolis|1=Letopolis}} (''Hem'', northwest of {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|qid=Q5715|lt=Memphis (Egypt)|1=Memphis, Egypt}}), embodied the season of sowing, which is why Kenmet was also designated as a recipient in the {{ill|Sacrifice|en|Sacrifice|qid=Q179723|lt=Sacrificial Cult|1=Sacrifice}}. Additionally, in this epoch, Kenmet was the {{ill|Chronocrat|de|Chronokrat|ja|クロノクラート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the first hour of the night and stood as the "armored one at the head of the divine {{ill|Star|en|Star|qid=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}}s during their rotation". == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian Deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|ja|エジプトの神々の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}} et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|ja|エジプト神と神名の辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Deities and Deity Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 90-429-1146-8, p. 290. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーベン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Book of Nut''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies et al., Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''The Sky over Esna – A Case Study on Religious Astronomy in Egypt as Exemplified by the Cosmological Ceiling and Architrave Inscriptions in the Temple of Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5. * {{ill|Joachim Friedrich Quack|en|Joachim Friedrich Quack|qid=Q1396207|lt=Joachim Friedrich Quack|1=Joachim Friedrich Quack}}, {{ill|Kim Ryholt|en|Kim Ryholt|qid=Q443108|lt=Kim Ryholt|1=Kim Ryholt}}: ''Hieratic texts from the collection - The Carlsberg papyri 7 -''.
